title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Testament of Ann Lee' starring Amanda Seyfried is streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' directed by Nia DaCosta is available on Netflix.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' is streaming on PBS featuring Jeff Goldblum and George Clooney.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox won their home opener 5-2 against the Padres, snapping a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin ended a 0-for-34 slump with an RBI single, earning cheers from Fenway Park fans.
Pitcher Johan Oviedo was placed on the 15-day injured list with an elbow strain, requires MRI evaluation.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots offseason workouts begin with phase one on April 20, focusing on conditioning and meetings.
The Patriots' OTA sessions are scheduled for May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
A mandatory three-day minicamp will take place from June 15-17, concluding the Patriots' voluntary offseason program.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's mock draft projects the Patriots selecting edge rushers Cashius Howell and Gabe Jacas in the first two rounds.
Texas A&M's Cashius Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season and is known for his explosive closing speed on quarterbacks.
Illinois' Gabe Jacas, a senior, concluded last season with 11 sacks and is praised for his run defense and play strength.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the San Diego Padres 5-2 during their home opener at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, while Willson Contreras hit a decisive home run over the Green Monster.
The win provided a needed morale boost for the team, which had a challenging 1-5 start to the season.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover seeks grant funding to implement a body-worn camera program after a shooting incident.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted of assault; her shooting highlighted the department's lack of body cameras.
State grants since 2021 have provided nearly $13 million to support police body camera programs.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park as a San Diego Padres player, acknowledging changes since leaving the Boston Red Sox.
Bogaerts received a standing ovation from Boston fans before his first at-bat, recognizing his contribution to two World Series titles.
Bogaerts discussed expectations for Red Sox rookie Roman Anthony, comparing Anthony's quick contract extension to his own career path.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Freedom Trail in Boston, a 2.5-mile path with 16 historical sites, attracts over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England introduces Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster opening April 11 for pass holders.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast offers prix fixe menus from April 9-18 at over 30 restaurants in New Hampshire.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and husband John McCauley appeal to Rhode Island Supreme Court over neighbor noise complaint.
Neighbors Avagyan and Beausoleil, along with tenant, accused of violating noise ordinance with industrial activities.
Supreme Court yet to decide on appeal; issue involves alleged zoning violations and harassment claims.

title: Delta is adding winter service between Boston and Rome in 2027
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Delta Air Lines will add winter flights between Boston and Rome starting in January 2027.
The service includes four weekly flights during winter, resuming daily flights in March 2027.
Tickets for the new Boston-Rome winter service go on sale April 4, 2026.
